slides.title: Forms & Expressions
slides.subtitle: Design data capture, validation, and calculated fields
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com > User guide > Forms & Expressions

slides.title: Form Designer Overview
bullets: Visual drag-and-drop form builder in process and dataform editors
Field palette on right — basic, advanced, and system fields
Form canvas in center — arrange fields in sections and rows
Section headers and dividers for logical grouping
Field properties panel — label, placeholder, required, default value
Preview form at any stage to validate user experience
slides.notes: Forms are shared between processes and dataforms — same designer.

slides.title: Basic Field Types
bullets: Text — single line and multi-line (textarea) input
Number — integer and decimal with min/max validation
Date and Date-time — calendar picker with format options
Dropdown — single select from predefined options list
Checkbox — boolean true/false and multi-select checkbox groups
Email, Phone, URL — formatted input with validation patterns
slides.notes: Demo adding each field type to a sample form.

slides.title: Advanced Field Types
bullets: Table/Grid — multi-row line items with computed columns
Lookup — reference data from other flows, dataforms, or datasets
User — select users from account directory
Attachment — file upload with size and type restrictions
Signature — digital signature capture on forms
Rich text — formatted text with images and links
slides.notes: Table fields are essential for PO lines, expense items, etc.

slides.title: Lookup Fields Deep Dive
bullets: Connect form fields to data in other Kissflow flows or dataforms
Configure source flow, filter conditions, and display columns
Auto-populate related fields when lookup value is selected
Lookup columns in tables — display lookup data in table rows
Cascading lookups — filter options based on parent field value
Also reference centralized Datasets for master data
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com — lookup columns in tables

slides.title: Field Validation Rules
bullets: Required field — prevent submission if empty
Min/max length for text, min/max value for numbers
Regex pattern matching for custom format validation
Unique value validation within a dataform
Conditional required — required only when another field has value
Custom validation messages for user-friendly error display
slides.notes: Show validation configuration in field properties.

slides.title: Expressions and Formulas
bullets: Expression builder for calculated and computed fields
Arithmetic: add, subtract, multiply, divide field values
String functions: concatenate, substring, uppercase, lowercase
Date functions: difference between dates, add days, format
Conditional expressions: IF/THEN/ELSE logic
Aggregate functions on table columns: SUM, COUNT, AVG, MIN, MAX
slides.notes: Demo: Total = Quantity × Unit Price in a table field.

slides.title: Conditional Visibility
bullets: Show or hide fields based on other field values
Example: show 'Rejection Reason' only when Status = Rejected
Multiple conditions with AND/OR logic
Apply to sections, individual fields, and table columns
Reduces form clutter — users see only relevant fields
Visibility rules evaluated in real-time as user fills form
slides.notes: Conditional visibility is one of the most powerful form features.

slides.title: Default Values and Auto-Population
bullets: Set static default values for fields
Dynamic defaults: current user, current date, logged-in department
Auto-populate from lookup selection
Pre-fill from URL parameters when form opened via link
Carry forward values from previous process item
System fields auto-populated: Created by, Created at, Item ID
slides.notes: Defaults reduce data entry burden and errors.

slides.title: AI Form Creation
bullets: Describe the form you need in natural language
AI generates fields, sections, and basic validation
Refine with visual form designer after generation
Accelerates prototyping — edit before publishing
Works for both process forms and dataform schemas
Enterprise AI with constraint-aware deterministic output

slides.title: Datasets — Centralized Reference Data
bullets: Datasets store master/reference data shared across the account
Import from CSV or populate via integrations
Use in lookup fields, reports, decision tables, and expressions
Governed by data policies for access control
Examples: country list, department codes, product catalog
Update dataset once — reflects everywhere it is referenced

slides.title: Decision Tables
bullets: Tabular business rules: input conditions → output actions
Define rules business users can maintain without code
Used in workflow conditions for routing and approval logic
Example: If Amount > 10000 AND Department = Finance → CFO approval
Import/export decision tables for backup and migration
Test decision table with sample inputs before deployment
slides.notes: Source: community.kissflow.com > User guide > Decision tables

slides.title: Forms Best Practices
bullets: Keep forms concise — only ask for necessary information
Group related fields into clearly labeled sections
Use lookups instead of free text for standardized data
Apply conditional visibility to reduce cognitive load
Test with real users before go-live — observe completion time
Document field purposes for future maintainers
